hq: 222 Montgomery Street (coworking space — no physical office owned)

system_description.overview: Knowtex uses GenAI to parse doctor-patient conversations into actions and insight. The platform is an AI-automated operating platform designed to help clinicians automate manual workflows including documentation, coding, and orders. It performs data synthesis and integration.

products.Automated Workflow: End-to-end workflow automation for clinician tasks including documentation, coding, and orders; integration and customization within client (EHR) environments
products.Admin Dashboard: Internal platform for analysis, metrics, and reporting; provided to customer admin to monitor utilization, accuracy, and data generated by clinicians
products.Clinician Support Services: White-glove onboarding, EHR implementation, and dedicated customer support

infrastructure.cloud_provider: AWS (carve-out — physical security delegated to AWS as subservice organization)
regions: Not explicitly named — single region implied from diagram
infrastructure.physical_presence: None — operates from coworking space at 222 Montgomery Street; no owned physical servers; all data on cloud
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ — 'multiple availability zones' referenced for failover
serverless: AWS App Runner (multiple instances visible in network diagram)

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Segmented VPCs with ACLs; customer access is segmented from other customer accounts (CC6.1.3); DB security group allowing traffic only from Bastion SG
network_architecture.api_gateway: AWS App Runner used for API/application serving (3 App Runner instances visible in network diagram with specific ARN URLs)
network_architecture.firewalls: Firewalls configured on application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols, and services; firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management (CC6.6.1)
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS via AWS — 'Knowtex Inc. employs IDS and IPS via AWS to identify and protect against threats in conjunction with its security policy network settings'
network_architecture.vpn: Bastion host (EC2) in public subnet for VPN/remote access; Systems Manager used for DB tunnel with IAM Auth
network_architecture.tls: TLS with certificates from established third-party certificate authority
network_architecture.content_filtering: Proxy server — all systems restricted to internet through content filtering system routed through proxy server
network_architecture.topology: Knowtex Web App and Admin Dashboard → API Request → 3x AWS App Runner instances → VPC Connector → VPC with Postgres (RDS) in private subnet; Bastion (EC2) in public subnet for admin DB access via Systems Manager tunnel with IAM Auth; DB SG allows traffic from Bastion SG only

application_architecture.pattern: Microservices / serverless-forward — multiple App Runner instances serving different functions (backend service, staging, admin)
application_architecture.api_style: REST (implied from web app + API request pattern)
application_architecture.environments: Separate development/test and production environments; changes tested in separate dev/test environment before production (CC8.1.4)

databases: PostgreSQL (AWS RDS — visible in network diagram as 'Postgres (RDS)')
data_storage.backup_frequency: Backup policy established; formal backup and recovery procedures; frequency not explicitly stated
data_storage.per_tenant_segregation: Customer access segmented from other customer accounts (CC6.1.3) — implies tenant-level access controls

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: AWS IAM (used for DB access via Systems Manager tunnel); Active Directory referenced for server accounts; password policy via AWS
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for user accounts with administrative access to production platform (CC6.1.1); MFA required for source code tool access (CC8.1.3); 2FA required for employee email access
authentication_access_control.access_revocation_sla: Within 1 business day of termination — 'user accounts being disabled within one business day upon termination of employment' (CC6.1.4)
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Via AWS password policy; periodic forced changes, password expiry, complexity requirements; account disabled after limited failed logon attempts; no guest/anonymous logins; unattended desktop lockout

encryption.in_transit: TLS (version not specified) with certificates from established third-party CA
encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service used to encrypt data at rest (CC6.1.5)
encryption.key_management: AWS KMS — 'cloud provider key management service to encrypt data at rest and to store and manage encryption keys'; access to production access keys restricted to authorized individuals

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Git-based SCM tool — 'source code management tool' referenced; code repository branch rules configured (CC8.1.2); access restricted via MFA (CC8.1.3)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Code repository branch rules configured to ensure every merge request to production requires additional approval; developers cannot make production code changes without approval (CC8.1.2)
ci_cd_devops.iac: Configuration management tool used for baseline configuration implementation and rollback capability (CC7.1.1) — 'baseline configurations are retained within the infrastructure as a code'
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: CTO/Management sets up automatic patch management; monthly patch management reviews; workstations configured for automatic updates; workstations scanned for patch compliance daily; patches tested for stability/availability before production deployment

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Not named — monitoring tool with predefined thresholds, incident invoked for confirmed events; performance monitoring covers disc space, incoming/outgoing network traffic, packet loss, CPU utilization
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Logging enabled for administrative activities, logon attempts, data deletions at application and infrastructure level, changes to functions, security configurations, permissions, and roles (CC7.2.1); access to log configuration and modification is restricted
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts configured to notify IT management of high and critical risk events; issues resolved via incident management process

third_party_services.purpose: Primary cloud infrastructure (IaaS) — App Runner, RDS, EC2, VPC, IAM, KMS, Systems Manager; subservice organization under carve-out method
Integration targets for clinician workflow automation

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and malware protection installed on all production servers (continuous auto-scan; definitions updated weekly on production servers); also on Mac workstations/laptops; endpoint security solution with log review by IT Head
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications; scan frequency adjusted as required; high/critical vulnerabilities remediated per incident response plan
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration test by independent third party; high/critical findings remediated and tracked to resolution (CC4.1.1)

bcdr.daily_backups: Backup policy established but frequency not explicitly stated as daily

excluded_criteria: CC6.4 — Physical facility access controls excluded (AWS responsibility as subservice org; Knowtex has no physical servers)
A1.2 — Environmental protections excluded (Knowtex operates from coworking space; physical/environmental controls are building management and AWS responsibility)
Processing Integrity — determined not relevant to the system
Privacy — determined not relevant to the system
notable_controls: CC8.1.3 — Source code tool access restricted to authorized users via MFA (explicitly named control)
CC6.1.3 — System components configured so company and customer access is segmented from other customer accounts (multi-tenancy isolation)
CC6.1.4 — 1-business-day access revocation SLA explicitly stated in control
CC6.2.2 — Quarterly user access reviews for ALL user accounts including those with admin privileges
CC4.1.1 — Annual external penetration test; high/critical findings tracked to resolution
CC7.1.1 — Infrastructure as Code (IaC) for baseline configuration with rollback capability
CC9.2.1 — Quarterly vendor risk assessments for critical vendors lacking SOC reports; annual SOC report review for vendors that have them
CC7.4.1 — BCDR tested annually against multiple defined loss scenarios; results reviewed and plans updated
CC2.1.4 — CISO role assigned; defined roles/responsibilities for security oversight in the incident response function

infrastructure.rationale: Multi-AZ confirmed, VPC with segmentation, IaC for config management, App Runner (managed container service). Single region. No CDN, no WAF. Real network diagram showing actual architecture is a strong positive vs. peers.

application_architecture.rationale: AWS App Runner for compute (managed, auto-scaling), Postgres RDS for database, VPC Connector, Systems Manager for secure DB access — actual technology names disclosed from network diagram. Separate environments, branch protection, MFA for source code access. No languages/frameworks named explicitly.

data_layer.rationale: PostgreSQL (RDS) confirmed in diagram. Backup policy exists with formal procedures. AWS KMS for encryption at rest. Data classification and retention policies in place. Tenant data segmentation addressed. No caching layer, no data warehouse mentioned. Backup frequency not explicitly stated as daily.

security_posture.rationale: Annual external pen test, monthly automated vuln scans, IDS/IPS via AWS, anti-virus on production servers (weekly definitions), endpoint protection on workstations, MFA on admin access and source code, firewall with annual review, content filtering via proxy, quarterly access reviews, 1-day revocation SLA. No WAF named. No cybersecurity insurance mentioned.

devops_maturity.rationale: IaC confirmed (baseline configs as code with rollback), App Runner (container-based managed deployment), branch protection with MFA for SCM access, emergency change process, separate environments, monthly patch reviews with automatic patch management, daily workstation compliance scanning. CI/CD tool not named.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy exists, tested annually against multiple loss scenarios, multi-AZ failover. DR plan covers BIA, roles, critical systems, and time frames. No multi-region failover. No RTO/RPO disclosed. Backup frequency not stated as daily.

vendor_diversity.rationale: AWS confirmed as primary with multiple services named. EHR integrations implied but EHR vendors not named. Certificate authority unnamed. Vendor management program with quarterly assessments for non-SOC vendors is mature. Overall still heavily AWS-concentrated.

overall.rationale: Knowtex is the standout among the ACCORP-templated Type 1 reports: it is a signed document, includes a real network diagram revealing actual AWS architecture (App Runner + Postgres RDS + Bastion + Systems Manager), handles sensitive healthcare PHI, and has notably mature controls for a Type 1 report (annual external pen test, monthly vuln scans, MFA on source code, quarterly access reviews). The healthcare use case (doctor-patient conversations via GenAI) creates significant HIPAA/PHI risk surface. No Processing Integrity or Privacy criteria included despite handling sensitive clinical data is a concern.

red_flags: Healthcare AI processing doctor-patient conversations — handles PHI — but Processing Integrity and Privacy trust criteria explicitly excluded from scope as 'not relevant to the system'
SOC 2 Type 1 only — point-in-time design assessment; no operating effectiveness testing
Single region — no multi-region failover for a healthcare-critical application
No WAF despite being an API-first healthcare data platform
yellow_flags: No RTO or RPO disclosed despite handling clinical workflow data where availability is critical
No CI/CD pipeline named — deployment process relies on App Runner but tooling unspecified
EHR integration targets unnamed — concentration risk on key integration dependencies not assessed
Backup frequency not explicitly stated as daily (policy exists but cadence not confirmed)
Signed by CTO only — no CEO signature on management assertion
green_flags: Real network diagram with actual AWS architecture visible — App Runner, Postgres RDS, Bastion EC2, VPC Connector, Systems Manager IAM Auth — highest technical disclosure among this batch
Management assertion actually signed (Jocelyn Kang, CTO, July 20, 2025) — unlike CoPlane and Reftab
Annual external penetration test by independent third party — explicitly named in controls
Monthly automated vulnerability scans with remediation tracking
MFA required for source code tool access (CC8.1.3) — strong DevOps security control
IaC with rollback capability for all baseline configurations
1-business-day access revocation SLA explicitly stated in control
Quarterly user access reviews including admin-privileged accounts
Quarterly vendor risk assessments for critical vendors without SOC reports
Anti-virus on production servers with weekly definition updates
Content filtering via proxy server for all internet access
BCDR tested annually against multiple defined loss scenarios with plan updates
AWS KMS for encryption key management explicitly named
key_observations: Knowtex is the only company in this batch with a fully completed, signed management assertion — the CTO signature and specific system description title suggest genuine engagement with the SOC process rather than checkbox compliance

The network diagram is genuine and reveals the full AWS architecture: 3 App Runner instances (CDK-deployed infrastructure), Postgres RDS in a private subnet accessible only via Bastion/Systems Manager, VPC Connector bridging App Runner to VPC — this is a modern serverless-forward architecture

The healthcare use case creates unique risk: GenAI parsing doctor-patient conversations processes PHI. The exclusion of Privacy and Processing Integrity criteria is legally questionable under HIPAA — BAA terms with AWS are not mentioned in the report
The proxy server for internet content filtering is unusual and sophisticated for a company of this size — suggests deliberate security architecture rather than default settings
AWS App Runner as the compute platform (rather than EC2 or ECS) indicates modern, managed containerized deployment with automatic scaling — a positive signal for operational maturity
The report uses ACCORP PARTNERS template for the controls section (CC1-CC9) but the system description section is genuinely company-specific and detailed — better quality than CoPlane or Reftab
No mention of HIPAA compliance, BAA agreements, or HITECH — notable gap for a healthcare data processor
